153114123.content:
"Oh, sounds like someone's getting hungry," Peach observed as she held up her breasts from the bottom, a teasing glint shining in her eye and a coy smirk at the corner of her mouth. "Are you hungry, little brother? Are you sure you're really hungry?"

"Yes, yes, I'm hungry!" Lemon shouted, bobbing in place in his guardian's cleavage. "Come on, big sis, let's sit down already! Please!? I'm hungry!"

"Now now, Lemon, you're gonna get your food," Peach said teasingly, making Lemon's eagerness fade away and replaced with uncertainty. The boy could only watch as Peach adjusted her breasts with one arm and reach for him with her other white-gloved hand. Plucking him out of her cleavage, Peach held up her little brother to her face with a mischievous smirk. "In fact, you might as well get your food while being food yourself."

"What!?" Lemon wriggled in his big sister's gloved grip while she gently opened her mouth and revealed the dark cave deep within her maw. A gust of warm morning breath washed over the puny panicking prince as Peach teasingly rolled her tongue across her plump lips. "Big sis, stop it! Don't tease me like this!"

"Why not? It's breakfast time, and the only thing that can satisfy my hunger is a little lemon." Peach giggled at her own awful joke and opened her maw once again, letting it hang while she dangled the little prince above it. "I could just drop you in right now, roll you all around my tongue and the inside of my mouth and send you straight to that "special world" inside my tummy."

Lemon began to cry hysterically at the frightening thought of being swallowed by his guardian, but he hardly found relief when Peach closed her pillow lips and spread them in a sly smirk. "But then again, I wonder if any of the breakfast food will be enhanced with just a little taste of Lemon. Let's find out, shall we?"

Peach gently tossed Lemon up in the air and watched him fall somewhere on the breakfast table, falling headfirst in one of the dishes that served a part of the breakfast meal. Suddenly, she heard the clicking of heels approaching the patio door, and Daisy, Rosalina, and Pauline emerged. "Hi, we made it here for breakfast!" Daisy profoundly announced in her signature outdoor voice. "Did we make it in time!?"

"Actually, you made it right on time!" Peach said as she got up and hugged her dear friends. "How are you doing, ladies?"

"We are fine, thank you," Rosalina said in her signature, maternally calm voice. She looked around the patio in worry. "Isn't Lemon with you?"

"He normally is, but he is running late," Peach fibbed, the urge to grin wickedly withheld behind an innocent, angelic demeanor. "He'll be part of the breakfast soon."

"Enough waiting around, let's eat!" Daisy said curtly, but excitedly.

"I agree. I'm starving," Pauline said eagerly. "I could eat a whole dinosaur right about now."

With Peach's permission, the ladies gathered at the table and took their seats. Before she could, she quickly moved her gaze around the table to see where Lemons had landed from her little toss-up. Her eyes darted to a tiny figure struggling in one of the dishes, and she giggled like a schoolgirl when she saw Lemon struggling in that particular dish, and the place where she randomly tossed him at couldn't have been in a better place.

"What's so funny?" Pauline asked.

"Oh, nothing," Peach childishly said. "I just enjoy these formal meetings with you girls."

"Yeah, but I wish Lemon was here to join us for breakfast," Daisy said. "He's always the life of the party."

Peach couldn't help but think that Daisy was closer to the truth than she or any of the girls realized as they dug into their breakfasts, unknowingly subjecting Lemon to a brand new slew of troubles as they reached for the dish that contained the little prince inside.

The only question is, which dish did was Lemon stuck in and at who's place at the table?

1.) Inside a teapot full of chamomile tea at Peach's place.
2.) Inside a bowl of loaded scrambled eggs at Daisy's place.
3.) Inside a bowl of mixed berries at Rosalina's place.
4.) Inside an empty coffee mug at Pauline's place.
5.) Inside a box of cereal at the center of the table.
153114123.title: Lemon for Breakfast

1531141235.content:
At the center of the table, there was a small box of Mari-O's, a circular-shaped oat cereal endorsed by none other than Lemon's Uncle Mario. When Peach tossed Lemon at the table, he fell headfirst through the cardboard flaps of the giant monolith filled with the cereal and landed with a splash.

Fortunately, the crumbling state of the O-shaped cereal broke his fall with a series of soft crunches, but it didn't make the landing any softer. Each piece of cereal was the size of a hula hoop, but they were as thick as a truck tire. Lemon was hurt and slightly disoriented from the landing, and watched as the flaps naturally returned to their original crease, trapping him in pitch darkness with the smell of processed cardboard and oats filling his lungs.

Lemon could hear the muffled voices of his adoptive sister and her close friends from outside, and he was too small to climb out. He wanted to scream for help, but none of the ladies outside would be able to hear him because of his size.

All of a sudden, Lemon felt the entire box shift as it was tilted towards the opening of the box where giant, immaculately manicured feminine fingers ripped the cardboard flaps open, blinding Lemon with light while he and the cereal were dumped into a large white bowl.

Meanwhile, the normal-sized women present were all busy chatting amongst themselves, completely unaware of Lemon’s plight within their cereal bowls, except for Peach, who instigated her little brother’s troubles. Peach had only poured her serving size portion of the cereal when the attention turned to the cereal served during the breakfast.

“You know, I’m curious, Peach,” Daisy spoke up. “You have all of these breakfast options, and you want to start with a bowl of cereal? What about the other items that don’t get eaten?”

“I’d like to think of this portion of cereal as a breakfast hors d'oeuvre,” Peach said. “Besides, anything we don’t eat, it all goes to the rest goes to the Toad orphanage so they will have something good to eat

“That’s delightful to hear,” Rosalina commended. “A good ruler always puts their subjects first and provides the needy the necessary sustenances, especially the needy children.”

“Actually, the whole thing is Lemon’s idea,” Peach clarified. “I guess it’s because he was a hungry orphan himself and doesn’t want the same fate to fall any needy child in the Mushroom Kingdom. He’s so sweet that way.”

“Indeed,” Pauline concurred. “I just wish he was a part of our breakfast. I just want to kiss him on account of how sweet he is.”

If you only knew, Peach thought impishly as she and the other girls poured their cereal, followed by the pouring of milk afterwards.

Lemon was buried at the bottom underneath an avalanche of oat cereal, crushed between a pile of dried oat rings and white, smooth porcelain. Then all of a sudden, his vision went white and watery as milk was poured into the cereal. Lemon swallowed a bit of the milk, but he was trapped underneath all that cereal, he felt like he could drown. Fortunately, thanks to Uncle Mario's swimming lessons, Lemon quickly swam through the milky depths and swam his way through the labyrinth of of Mari-O's, until he popped out of the surface, choking for air and coughing up the milk.

The tiny-born prince swam over to the side of a Mari-O for buoyancy. When he looked around, he realized, to his horror, that he was trapped in the center of a Mari-O. Lemon could not have picked a worse place to come up for air, especially when a giant metal spoon dipped so close to him, scooping up a giant pile of cereal and milk in one go.

Lemon watched that spoon lift up its contents and brought to the open mouth of the giantess eating her cereal. The lips closed and pulled the spoon out cleanly. Her jaws moved upwards and downwards, from side to side, compacting and crunching the cereal while swirling it around with the milk, turning it to mush. His blood froze as she witnessed the giantess's throat bob with a guttural swallow. He shuddered at the thought that might be the one being sent down that throat.

A shadow moved over Lemon's position as the spoon was dipped into the bowl, guided by the giant hand of ______ which scooped up the cereal and milk only inches away from the prince.

1.) Peach (aware)
2.) Daisy
3.) Rosalina
4.) Pauline
1531141235.title: A Bowl of Cereal with Lemon

15311413.content:
“Auntie Peach,” Lemon said softly with a smile.

Little Lemon was the prince and only son of the widowed queen of the Tiny-Huge Lands. After his mother was eaten by a giant fish at sea, Lemon was left orphaned. Peach, who was a very close friend with Lemon's mother, offered to take the boy in and raise him. In the meantime, the queen of Tiny-Huge Land had a cousin who would rule the kingdom in her stead until Lemon came of age.

Peach got up to stretch and set Lemon on a counter so she could continue her morning stretches. Lemon mirrored her actions the best he could until the older blonde was done. The two royals then got dressed and made their way to the dining hall. The two royals were eating breakfast which consisted of muffins. “What are we going to do today, Auntie?” Lemon asked with a smile as he looked up at his surrogate aunt.

Right before Peach could say anything, there was a loud explosion, followed by a loud laugh. “Gahahahaha! Queen Bowsette here to conquer!”

Standing in the hole of the wall was Bowsette herself. While peace was actually sustained between the Koopas and Mushroom Kingdom, it didn’t take long for something to go wrong. Turns out the item known as the Super Crown came from another planet ruled by Bowsette, who was a separate being from Bowser. Bowsette shared the Koopa King's desire to conquer other kingdoms when she heard of Lemon and thought the boy would make a great prize.

Bowsette spotted Lemon on the table and managed to rush over at surprising speed and snatched the boy before hopping onto an airship and escaping. “Noooo!” Peach cried out as the young boy was taken away.

What is Bowsette’s body type?

1. Just like the original depiction in her first appearance, tall and buxom with blonde hair.

2. Taking after her namesake, Bowsette is huge, as large as the Koopa King himself, maybe even a bit bigger.

Bowsette was indeed the humanized, female version of her Koopa counterpart. She was as big as Bowser in terms of both muscles and size (maybe even bigger), she shared the same physical qualities (including the same shell, tail, thick eyebrows, red hair, and eye color), she commanded legions of enemy troops, and she was all-around evil.

The thick, muscular giantess inserted a crying, wailing Lemon into her cleavage and secured him tight between her massive valley. "There's no use crying for your auntie now, Lemon," she said with a deep, feminine butch voice. "You're going to be my personal toy for the rest of your life, so you might as well enjoy the ride on the luxurious S.S. Bowsette airship while you still can."

"Please, no! Auntie!" Lemon cried out before a massive bronze finger pushed him in deeper, silencing him.

The airship that carried the evil queen and her young prisoner flew through the skies of the Mushroom Kingdom until they arrived to a craggy, volcanic area where Bowsette's Castle resided. It resembled Bowser's Castle, but it prominently featured Bowsette's face. The airship landed in the courtyard, and Bowsette disembarked, passing by the lineup of monsters who all bowed as she passed them by.

"I'll be in my room for the rest of the day," Bowsette informed the armored Koopatrols. "Bring my meals up to my room, but don't disturb me, or you're gonna get more than a slight burn. Got it?"

"Yes, ma'am!" the two guards saluted.

Bowsette walked inside and swung the heavy metal doors open, allowing them to slam shut behind her.

The Koopa Queen entered her bedroom and locked the door, making sure to put up a "Do Not Disturb" sign on the door for good measure. She reached into her cleavage and pulled out Prince Lemon, the young royal choking for fresh air as he dangled in the muscular giantess's sharp nails. "Heh. Looks like you got a bit winded from your ride over," Bowsette taunted. "Too bad for you, because you're not gonna get a single breath of relief with me around."

"Wh-What are you gonna do to me!?" Lemon whimpered.

"Oh, there's plenty I want to do to you, my sweet little sour snack," Bowsette said, licking her chops. "The trouble is, I just don't know where to start. I mean you're just so little, young, and adorable, and I just seem like a hulking, menacing monster to you. To tell you the truth, it turns me on to see a young prince cower under my size, but just thinking about the many ways to torture you just makes me wanna reach for my special area..."

Bowsette reached for her crotch and began to gently stroke it, turned on by the many scenarios of how to toy with the little prince. He was her prize, but she wasn't sure how to use him. She could try to subject him to humiliations under her feet and other body parts, or just taste him and eat him how she wants to. The possibilities were endless!

15312.content:
The Legend of Zelda series is one of Nintendo’s staple series that’s broadened it’s own horizons since the original game was released in the early 1980’s.

The original Legend of Zelda features a top-down view of the game’s iconic hero, Link, as he explores the overworld of Hyrule and dungeons to rescue the fair Princess Zelda from the clutches of Ganon by restoring the Triforce of Wisdom. The game became such a huge success that Nintendo launched the titular game franchise that video game fans know and love, with iconic releases such as A Link to the Past, Ocarina of Time, and Breath of the Wild not only driving the franchise to success, but also becoming significant milestones in the development of video games as a whole.

Decades after the launch of the first Legend of Zelda game, Nintendo released an official timeline in their book, Hyrule Historia, explaining the chronological order of the game series, from Hyrule’s foundation in Skyward Sword to the timelines diverging into three separate timelines during the events of Ocarina of Time.

The first timeline, The Fallen Hero Timeline, contains the events of Zelda games featuring the series’s overhead view functions, such as A Link to the Past, Link’s Awakening, and A Link Between Worlds. The second timeline, the Child Timeline, contains the events of Majora’s Mask, the sequel to Ocarina of Time (although,we will merge both games into one route), Twilight Princess, and Four Swords Adventure. The third timeline, the Adult Timeline containing the events of Zelda games featuring Toon Link, including The Wind Waker, it’s sequel The Phantom Hourglass, and Spirit Tracks.


The only question is, which timeline will you choose to follow?

Unified Timeline (i.e. Skyward Sword, The Minish Cap, Ocarina of Time/Majora's Mask)

Fallen Hero Timeline (i.e. A Link to the Past, Link’s Awakening, A Link Between Worlds, Oracle series)

Child Timeline (i.e. Twilight Princess, Four Swords Adventure)

-or-

Adult Timeline (i.e. Wind Waker, Phantom Hourglass, Spirit Tracks)
15312.title: The Legend of Zelda series

153121.content:
This timeline contains the events of The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword, The Minish Cap, and Ocarina of Time in chronological order.

Prior to the events of Skyward Sword, the three Golden Goddesses - Din, Goddess of Power; Nayru, Goddess of Wisdom; and Farore, Goddess of Courage - descended upon the chaos of the universe and created the realm of what would be known later as Hyrule. Din created the material realm, Nayru gave the realm order and law, and Farore created life to follow the order. When the world was created and their labors done, the three Golden Goddess left behind a piece of their essence in the form of three golden triangles called the Triforce and left it in the careful hands of the Goddess Hylia before departing into the heavens.

After the creation of the realm, the Demon King Demise sought to wield the power of the Triforce, so he led a horde of demons onto the realm to wrest it from Hylia's hands. To prevent this, Hylia gathered the remaining humans of the land and sent an outcropping of the land into the sky where the Triforce was hidden inside the floating outcrop above the cloudy barrier. Hylia and the five tribes of the land battled Demise and sealed him away where the outcrop used to be, but the Goddess suffered many injuries. She tasked the spirit within the Goddess Sword, Fi, to assist her chosen champion before she was reborn in the form of a mortal girl, the first Zelda in the floating outcropping called Skyloft.

In Skyward Sword, Zelda was forced from the sky by a tornado conjured up by Demise's servant, Ghirahim, prompting Zelda's childhood friend, Link to find her. After Link meets Fi, the spirit residing within the Goddess Sword, he is tasked to obtain the gifts of the three Sacred Flames to bless the Goddess Sword into the Master Sword. After Zelda reveals herself to be Hylia's mortal form and was put into a deep slumber, Link is tasked to gather parts of the "Song of the Hero" from the Goddess's appointed dragons to unlock the entrance to the Sky Keep where the Triforce laid hidden. The Hero eventually reaches the Triforce and sends Skyloft plummeting into the ground, destroying Demise's sealed form, The Imprisoned.

Just as Zelda reawakens, she is kidnapped by Ghirahim and sent to the past where the Demon Lord successfully drains Hylia's essence into The Imprisoned, absorbing Zelda's essence and reawakening Demise. After a victorious battle against the Demon King, Demise curses Link and Zelda's later incarnations into a never-ending cycle against Demise's mortal forms before he was destroyed. Link and Zelda return to the present, and the land of Hyrule was established.

Prior to the events of The Minish Cap, the land of Hyrule was infested by evil spirits who were turning the land of Hyrule into darkness. Just as the world seemd to be cast in shadow, the tiny humanoid race, the Picoris/Minish descended from the skies and bestowed a courageous hero the Light Force and the Picori Blade. The Hero of Men used his wisdom and courage, along with his gifts to defeat the demons and restore peace in the world.

In The Minish Cap, Link's childhood friend, Princess Zelda is sealed in stone by the Sorcerer of Winds, Vaati during a tournament in the midst of the celebration of the Picori's first appearance in Hyrule. Vaati was formerly a Minish, an apprentice under the wise Minish sage, Ezlo, who created a magical wishing cap that granted the wearer their wishes. Vaati stole the magical cap, became a powerful sorcerer, turned Ezlo into a talking bird-like cap, and went to steal the Light Force form himself.

Tasked by the King of Hyrule to seek out the Picori's help, Link travels to Minish Woods where he meets Ezlo in his cap form, attacked by a group of Octoroks. After Link saves Ezlo, the former Minish sage helps Link travel to the Minish Village by shrinking down to the size of the Picori, and is given the task to reforce the Picor Blade by collecting the Four Elements, the embodiments of earth, air, fire, and water. After obtaining the Four Elements and fusing them with the Picori Blade inside the Elemental Sanctuary hidden within Hyrule Castle, the sacred Four Sword was reforged, and Link uses its duplication powers and his shrinking abilities to defeat Vaati's ghastly forms and seal him away in the Four Sword. Princess Zelda is released from her stone imprisonment and Hyrule is restored to peace.

In Ocarina of Time, the evil king of the Gerudos, Ganondorf, sought the Triforce by sending monsters to sicken Kokiri Forest, Death Mountain, and Zora's Domain where each of the locations kept hidden a Spiritual Stone to unlock the Master Sword sealed within the Temple of Time, the key to the gateway to the Sacred Realm where the Triforce was kept safe. Young Link, a young Hylian raised by the Kokiri, a race of forest children appointed with a fairy guardian, was given his fairy partner, Navi, to help save the land of Hyrule from Ganondorf.

When Link claimed the three Spiritual Stones by vanquishing Ganondorf's monsters, the King of Evil made his move by raiding Hyrule Castle. Link is given the Royal Family's most sacred treasure, the Ocarina of Time, by Princess Zelda as she rode to safety with her retainer, Impa. After Link gained access to the Master Sword inside the Temple of Time, Ganondorf followed Link into the Sacred Realm, claimed the Triforce, and plunged Hyrule into seven years of chaos. Meanwhile, Link grew to become seven years older, armed with the Master Sword, and tasked by Rauru, the Sage of Light to gather the six Sages of each race in Hyrule. Aided by the mysterious Sheik, Link gathers the Sages and defeats Ganondorf's monsters in each hidden Temple.

After the last Sage was rescued, Sheik reveals himself to be Princess Zelda in disguise right before she was kidnapped by Ganondorf and imprisoned within his castle where Hyrule Castle once stood. After a lengthy battle against the King of Evil, Link defeats Ganondorf, who is then sealed in the Dark Realm by the Sages. Guilty about being a part into Link's forced maturity as a child, Princess Zelda uses the Ocarina of Time to send Link back seven years to stop Ganondorf, while Navi flies away with her task completed.

In the sequel, Majora's Mask, Link is ambushed by two Fairies, Tatl and Tael, working with their partner, Skull Kid. While Skull Kid played with the Ocarina of Time gifted to Link by Princess Zelda, the child Hero of Time pursues Skull Kid into the parallel world of Termina where he is turned into a Deku Scrub by the effects of the eerie Majora's Mask stolen from the Happy Mask Salesman.

As part of an agreement, the Happy Mask Salesman tasks Link to retrieve his Ocarina of Time and Majora's Mask from the Skull Kid within three days' time in exchange that the Salesman would teach him a song to restore him back to his former form. Link, along with Tatl who was accidentally left behind by Skull Kid, aids Link in retrieving the Ocarina of Time, but discovers that Skull Kid was using the power of the mask to send the Moon to collide with Termina in three days' time. By repeatedly using the Ocarina of Time and traveling back to the first day, Link and Tatl travel to the four different areas in Termina's cardinal directions where they must defeat the Skull Kids' masked monsters and awaken the Four Giants to prevent the Moon from destroying the world.

After awakening the Four Giants, Link prevents the Moon from falling onto Termina while also breaking Majora's Mask's influence over the Skull Kid. Link then travels inside the Moon to defeat the three different forms of Majora and restore peace to Termina. Link and Skull Kid become friends, the Happy Mask Salesman leaves with the depowered Majora's Mask back in his hands, and Link ventures off to search for Navi, never heard from again.

(A/N: Although Majora's Mask takes place in the Child Timeline, it would make sense to merge both Ocarina of Time and Majora's Mask into a single route.)

The only question is, which game(s) will you choose?

Skyward Sword

The Minish Cap

-or-

Ocarina of Time/Majora's Mask
153121.title: Unified Timeline

1531213.content:
The boy is a character from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and its sequel, Majora's Mask.

The only question is, which of these scenarios will you apply to the young male characters in both games?

Size-Alteration: There are many ways a character from The Legend of Zelda series can shrink and grow. In one scenario, a boy character(s) finds a mushroom spore in a forested area which permanently shrinks anyone who comes into contact with the spore, but can be administered with an antidote. In another scenario, the characters have their sizes altered by magic, from boys within the age limit being shrunken by Majora's Mask to female characters growing to giant size by the Giant's Mask. In another scenario, the characters have their sizes affected by a size-alteration potion which spells disaster for whoever drinks it, even more so when the potion spills into the river, Hyrule's main freshwater supply.

Born Tiny: The young male character(s) are a fraction of the hundreds of children born tiny due to the genetic deficiency of the XY chromosome, so with a world full of predominantly female characters, they are doted on by the giant girls in their lives, or they are seduced by them. The lands of Hyrule and Termina is dangerous enough at normal size, but that danger at their size is multiplied a hundred fold.

Normal-Size Among Giantesses: The male character(s) are born normal-sized, but the female characters in the series are more massive than him. In one scenario, the boy is close friends with/raised by the Great Fairies, who are already gigantic in comparison to a normal-sized Hylian. In another scenario, after the events of Ocarina of Time and/or Majora's Mask, the women seem to be larger than normal size by an unknown form of magic, with the young girls being the size of the Goron chief, Darunia and the adults as large as a two-story house in Castle Town Square. In another scenario, the Gerudo become taller than their average normal adult size seen in both games, and they're looking to adopt/play with the male characters who enter their territory.

-or-

Space-Time Rift: The young male character(s) accidentally open or discover a rift in space of time that takes them to anywhere in Hyrule, or into a different era in the past for the future. The only problem is that the rift also has some nasty side-effects which makes the young male character(s) appear as small as a Minish on the other side of the rift and at the mercy of the gigantic female characters!
1531213.title: Ocarina of Time/Majora's Mask

15312131.content:
The characters from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and its sequel, Majora's Mask had their sizes altered.

The only question is, which of these scenarios will you apply to the young male characters in both games?

Shrinking Spores: There is a rare but dangerous mushroom growing in grasslands, forests, and woodlands called the Vaati's Cap. When touched, it released little spores which shrinks the character to the size of a Minish and turn their skin color to purple and their eyes red. There is an antidote that can be administered, but the process takes too much time and the ingredients are rare to find, so the character who inhaled the spores are permanently tiny.

Curse of the Masks: In Termina, the Masks contain special magical abilities that even linger in the outskirts of that parallel dimension. However, the Masks aren't to be used very lightly because doing so will create a magical drawback. In one scenario, Skull Kid uses Majora's Mask to shrink down a character (himself included) and is teleported somewhere in Termina or even Hyrule. In another scenario, the Giant's Mask is worn by a female character, and she interacts with the boy they're closely associated with. In another scenario, Link uses the Song of Healing to heal a wounded Minish and transforms his spirit into the Minish Mask, which he uses to shrink and explore his gigantic world.

Goddess-Sized Gerudo: In Hyrule, Koume and Kotake, the old twin witches who raised Ganondorf with the ability to merge into giant witch, Twinrova, exacts their revenge on Young Link and/or the youth of Hyrule by brainwashing the Gerudo, then using their magic to alter someone's size. They either shrink their victims and they become the Gerudos' plaything, or they expand the Gerudos and send them rampaging into Hyrule as an army of hypnotized giantesses. Either way, Twinrova will be assured both victory and the satisfaction of revenge.

-or-

Puny-Sized Potions or Enormous Elixirs: There is a potion brewed in either Hyrule or Termina which affects the size of the person who drinks it. In one scenario, a boy accidentally drinks the shrinking potion and he shrinks to tiny size in an inconvenient area. In another scenario, a girl accidentally drinks the growth potion and she grows into a towering giantess and interacts with the boy she's closely associated with. In another scenario, the potions are accidentally spilled into the river, and either the boys shrink or the girls grow when they drink it.
15312131.title: Size-Alteration

153121312.content:
The characters from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and its sequel, Majora's Mask had their sizes altered by the effects of the Masks.

The only question is, which which of these scenarios will you apply to the characters in both games?

Majora's Miniaturization Magic: When the Skull Kid put on the accursed Majora's Mask, he gained the power to utilize dark magical powers to cause dangerous mischief around Termina. At one point, the Skull Kid uses Majora's Mask to shrink a boy(s) that he targeted and teleported them to an area in Termina or beyond where the female characters are prominently seen. In one scenario, Majora's Mask strips itself from Skull Kid when the imp's usefulness came to an end and shrinks him down to size.

The Giant Mask's Girl Gigantification: The Giant's Mask is a mask that is only worn in the Stone Tower Temple in Termina, but somehow, when the Giant's Mask is worn by a female character in either Hyrule or Termina, it causes them to grow into a colossal giantess. Somewhere down the line, the Giant's Mask's magic allows its wearer to share some of its magical abilities into another female character. They then loom over the boy they're closely associated with, like Link, Mido or Kafei.

-or-

The Minish Mask: During his adventures in Termina, Young Link comes across a Picori/Minish mortally injured by one of the Skull Kid's cruel pranks when he compelled the Snappers to mutiliate the defenseless little creature. Using the Song of Healing, Link heals the Minish's spirit and transforms into the Minish Mask where he transforms into a tiny Minish that only children can see, but remain invisible to the adults.
153121312.title: Curse of the Masks

choices: The Bombers Secret Society of Justice
1531213121.content:
The character is a boy from The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask whose size has been altered by Majora's Mask.

The only question is, which of these characters is going to be shrunk by Majora's Mask?

Young Link: During his pursuit of the Skull Kid in the Lost Woods, Link stumbles into a certain tree trunk sending him into the underground catacombs connecting the Lost Woods to the parallel world of Termina. When he fell onto the Deku Flower, Link was then cursed by the Skull Kid wearing Majora's Mask, who shrinks him to showcase his power over him. He is then teleported by Majora's Mask to a place in Hyrule where one of his female friends is present.

Kafei: After a night of drinking and celebrating his engagement to Anju, an inebriated Kafei brushes the Skull Kid away, causing him to use Majora's Mask to shrink him down and teleports him to another area in Termina. He has three days to return to the Milk Bar where he met the Skull Kid or his change will be permanent.

Skull Kid: The Skull Kid has successfully used the power of Majora's Mask to wreak havoc across Termina. He engaged in criminal mischief, sealed away the Four Giants inside their own Temples, and summoned the Moon to crash into Termina in three days. As the third day arrived, when the Skull Kid's usefulness was finished, Majora's Mask suddenly strips itself away and shrinks him with his powers. He is left at the mercy of the giant women he wronged in Termina.

-or-

The Bombers Secret Society of Justice: Led by their Leader, Jim, the Bombers Secret Society of Justice is a small group of six boys who strive to do good in their home town of Clock Town. One day, Jim lets the Skull Kid join in on their fun, but they regret it when the Skull Kid's dark magic mask shrinks them down less than the size of an ant. The Bombers must work together to survive the hostile, giant world where even the Deku Princess is as large as one of the Four Giants themselves!
1531213121.title: Majora's Miniaturization Magic

1531213122.content:
The character is a girl from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and/or its sequel, Majora's Mask whose size has been altered by the Giant's Mask.

Since there are a lot of female characters in both games, it would make sense that this story route would feature two scenarios based around the female characters growing into a giant by the Giant's Mask, each one taking place in either Hyrule or Termina.

The only question is, which of these scenarios will you apply to the female characters of both games?

The Humongous Girls of Hyrule: After returning from his travels in Termina, Link and Princess Zelda catch up on their time apart from each other. When Link shows Zelda the Masks he collected in his journeys, Zelda curiously puts on the Giant's Mask and instantly becomes a masked giantess. At some points, Zelda transfers some of her powers to her friends and fellow Sages and they grow into giants themselves.

-or-

The Titanesses of Termina: During his travels in Termina, Link settles down for the night at Romani's Ranch where the eponymous red-haired girl curiously shuffles through his knapsack and pulls out the Giant's Mask. Romani quickly reaches giant size and has a little fun with her little "grasshopper", as well as the citizens of Clock Town. At some points, Romani transfers some of her powers to her older sister, Cremia, and her friends.
1531213122.title: The Giant Mask's Girl Gigantification

choices: The Bombers Secret Society of Justice
15312132.content:
The boy is a character from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and its sequel, Majora's Mask who was born small.

The only question is, which one of these boys will you choose?

Young Link: Link was born to a Hylian woman who perished at the roots of the Great Deku Tree and entrusted her son's safety to him and the Kokiri children. Growing up, Link would have many adventures in the Kokiri Forest often under the watchful protection of his guardian and Forest Sage, Saria, who like the other other Kokiri girls, watched over their tiny male charges. Link is close friends with Princess Zelda, Princess Ruto, and Malon, and is engaged in combat training and discipline under Impa, Nabooru, and the Gerudo. At some point, Link will accidentally find a rift in space and time that sends him into a different era of Hyrule, even in a parallel dimension like Termina.

Kafei: Kafei is the young son of Madame Aroma, the wife of the miniscule mayor of Clock Town in Termina. Despite being young and tiny, Kafei is currently engaged to the Stock Pot Innkeeper, Anju, and by their mothers' blessings, the two are set to be married during the Carnival of Time. Kafei also often interacts with Anju's friends, Cremia and Romani, the Indigo-Go's singer Lulu, and the Rosa Sisters. Because of his handsomeness, Kafei is the target of every girl's desires and will often try to snatch him right from under Anju's nose.

Skull Kids: The Skull Kids are male Hylian children who were transformed into creaky, straw-like imps by the mysterious magic of the Lost Woods. The Skull Kids are known for their love of madcap pranks, mischief, and music. Every so often, the tiny Skull Kids play with the Kokiri girls who venture into the Lost Woods and often play music. At other times, when they feel particularly mischievous, the Skull Kids call upon a bird friend of theirs to fly them to Hyrule where they hope to pull their mischeivous pranks on the unsuspecting giant women of Hyrule, including the Crown Princess Zelda herself!

The Kokiri Boys: While Link ventures into the Lost Woods or accompany Saria around Hyrule to spend time with his female friends, the Kokiri Boys are put under constant surveillance from the other Kokiri girls. Among them is Mido, Link's childhood rival and bully, and the intelligent Know-It-All Brothers, who wonder what it's like outside Kokiri Forest despite the dire threat of their death upon setting foot outside the forest's borders. In a secret group meeting, Mido and the Know-It-All Brothers plan to sneak out the next time Saria ventures outside the forest, but they'll soon discover how dangerous the outside world of Hyrule can be.

-or-

The Bombers Secret Society of Justice: Led by their Leader, Jim, the Bombers Secret Society of Justice is a small group of six boys who strive to do good in their home town of Clock Town. One day, Jim has an idea to spread the idea of justice around the land of Termina, despite how dangerous it is outside of Clock Town's guarded gates, which leads the boys into no small shortage of unintended mischief and trouble in every province of Termina (except for the Snowhead Mountains since no female characters exist up there).
15312132.title: Born Tiny

15312133.content:
The boy is a character from The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time and its sequel, Majora's Mask that's born normal-sized but is among larger female characters.

The only question is, which of these scenarios will you apply to the young male characters in both games?

Related to the Great Fairies: The young male character is related to the Great Fairies, the giant female humanoid form of the Fairies who are much larger in comparison to a normal-sized person. The boy is either close friends with one or all of the Great Fairies in the games, or he is an orphan who was raised by the Great Fairies as their surrogate son. The Great Fairies are jovial and jolly, which either fits well with the boy's character or completely contrasts it.

Girls Grown Wild: In the events of Ocarina of Time and/or Majora's Mask, the female characters seem to be larger than the young male characters, grown by an unknown form of magic. They have either grown into Amazonian size, or grown into the size of the Four Giants from Termina, leaving the young male characters feeling small and puny before their size and might. (A/N: The female characters' heights are variable, so long as the younger characters seen in either games has the minimum height of six feet or more).

-or-

Amazons of the Desert: Taking a page out of The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild, the Gerudo seen in Ocarina of Time and Majora's Mask are grown to Amazonian size, with the younger Gerudo the size of a normal-sized adult, but the adult Gerudo growing up to fifteen feet tall. With their size, they can adopt and/or dominate the male characters from both games, including the younger male characters.
15312133.title: Normal-Sized Among Giantesses

153123.content:
The Child Timeline contains the events of Twilight Princess, and Four Swords Adventure.

Prior to the events of Twilight Princess, in ancient times, a group of Hylians called the Dark Interlopers attempted to take control of Hyrule and use the Triforce for their own purposes using their dark powers. In response to this, the three Golden Goddesses - Din, Goddess of Power; Nayru, Goddess of Wisdom; and Farore, Goddess of Courage - called upon four light spirits from each province of Hyrule and sealed their power away in the Fused Shadow. Then the Dark Interlopers were banished into the Twilight Realm through the Mirror of Twilight where they adapted to the Twilight and became the race known as the Twili.

Many centuries later, after the events of Ocarina of Time, Ganondorf's intentions to dominate Hyrule and claim the Triforce were exposed, so he was captured, tried, and executed by the Six Sages. When the Six Sages used their sword to mortally wound Ganondorf, the Triforce of Power was activated, and Ganondorf broke free from his chains and immediately killed the Sage of Water. The Sages, fearful of this power, banished Ganondorf into the Twilight Realm where his spirit aided the power-hungry Zant into giving him the power to turn the Twili into mindless beasts, usurp the throne from the Twilight Princess Midna, and send Hyrule and the World of Light into darkness.

In Twilight Princess, two centuries after Ocarina of Time, Link's home province of Ordona was invaded by monsters and the Twilight Beasts. He ventured into the twilight where he is forcibly transformed into a wolf, catching the attention of Midna in her cursed imp form, wearing a chunk of her Fused Shadow. In this form, Wolf Link meets Princess Zelda who tasks him and Midna to retrieve the Fused Shadows and restore light to Hyrule with the aid of the light spirits. After collecting the Fused Shadow, Zant forces Midna into the World of Light, mortally wounding her until she is healed by Zelda at the cost of her own spirit.

Link and Midna venture to the Sacred Grove where Link pulls the Master Sword from its resting place in the ruins of the Temple of Time. He then journeys around Hyrule to retrieve the Mirror of Twilight to reopen the gateway into the Twilight Realm where they vanquish Zant, who reveals Ganondorf has returned to Hyrule. With the power of the Fused Shadow, Link and Midna journey into Hyrule Castle and defeat Ganondorf's spirit, who possessed Zelda's body and transformed into a beast. In a final battle against the King of Evil, Link plunges the Master Sword into Ganondorf's heart and kills him, breaking the curse over Midna, revealing her true, beautiful form. Midna departs from the World of Light and the Mirror of Twilight shatters.

In Four Swords Adventure, Princess Zelda calls upon her six Maidens and attempts to enforce the seal on the sword, but the shadowy form of Link kidnaps Zelda and the Maidens, and provokes Link to pull the Four Sword from its pedestal. In doing so, Link not only is split into four separate Links, but he breaks the Sorcerer of Winds, Vaati from his seal, freeing him.

The four Links travel across Hyrule and work together to stop the chaos caused by Shadow Link and Vaati, where they also discover that Vaati has been resurrected and used by the King of Darkness, Ganon. Ganon was once a man named Ganondorf who stole the legendary Trident which gave him the power of darkness, as well as the ability to spawn Shadow Links as resentment from being defeated by Link in Twilight Princess. After rescuing the six Maidens, the four Links confront Vaati in the Palace of Winds where Ganon reveals himself as the true mastermind behind the chaos.

With the combined efforts of the four Links, Princess Zelda, and the six Maidens, Ganon is sealed inside the Four Sword and laid to rest again. The four Links merge back into the singular Link, who is hailed by Hyrule as a hero.

The only question is, which of these games will you follow?

Twilight Princess

-or-

Four Swords Adventure
153123.title: Child Timeline

153125.content:
This route accounts for the different Legend of Zelda games that are either unclear where in the official timeline the games belong to, games featuring ambiguously canon content or spin-offs, or games that have yet to be released.

The Great Calamity timeline remains unclear where in the official Legend of Zelda timeline it belongs, seeing as it contains multiple references to the different timelines in the previous chapter, so for the sake of argument, this timeline will be separated into its own category, containing events from Breath of the Wild, it’s ambiguously canon prequel, Hyrule Warriors: Age of Calamity, and the upcoming sequel.

While we're on the subject of ambiguously canon (or non-canon games), there are a few Legend of Zelda spin-off games to account for that don't belong in the official timeline, such as Hyrule Warriors, Cadence of Hyrule, and possibly even more. (A/N: Super Smash Bros. will not be included in this route, as it has its own route.)

The only question is, which route will you follow?

The Great Calamity Timeline (i.e. Breath of the Wild, Hyrule Warriors: Age of Calamity, or the upcoming sequel)

-or-

Spin-Offs (i.e. Hyrule Warriors, Cadence of Hyrule, etc.)
153125.title: Other

1531251.content:
This timeline contains the events of The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild, its upcoming sequel, and the ambiguously canon Hyrule Warriors: Age of Calamity.

Prior to the events of Breath of the Wild, according to legend, Calamity Ganon is the primal source of evil that's plagued Hyrule for over ten thousand years. With the aid of the Sheikah's ancient weaponry, the Guardians, and the four pilotable Divine Beasts designed to combat Calamity Ganon, the princess who carries the blood of the goddess and the hero who possesses the spirit of the hero sealed the Calamity away.

One hundred years prior to the events of Breath of the Wild, Princess Zelda struggled to fulfill her duties to awaken her powers to seal away the foreseen return of the Calamity, and her animosity towards her chosen knight, Link, whose possession of the Master Sword reminded her of her failures. The Sheikah technology had been unearthed, as well as the Guardians and the Divine Beasts. Each of the Divine Beasts were piloted by a Champion of the other four races governing Hyrule: the Rito, the Gorons, the Zoras, and the Gerudo. With the Champions assembled, victory seemed to be at hand.

Unfortunately, Zelda's inability to awaken her power accelerated Ganon's return to Hyrule. Using his malice, the Calamity infected the Guardians and sent Blights to infect each of the Divine Beasts and kill their chosen pilots. As the infected Guardians were about to destroy Link and Zelda, the princess's power awakened as the mortally injured hero laid dying. Compelled by the voice of the Master Sword, Zelda ordered the Sheikah to place Link into the Shrine of Resurrection and put the sword into a slumber before the roots of the Great Deku Tree. With her power, Zelda sealed herself and Ganon within Hyrule Castle and waited until Link's return in the next one hundred years.

In Breath of the Wild, Link wakes up to a beautiful voice half-naked, having no memory of his placement as per the effects of the Shrine of Resurrection. The story of Hyrule was imparted to himt by the ghost of King Rhoam Bosphoramus Hyrule, who urged Link to seek out Impa, the Sheikah elder, and save his daughter, Zelda. At Impa's urge, Link sought after fragments of his memories, traveled the land of Hyrule to destroy the Blights infecting the Divine Beasts, and freed them along with the ghosts of their pilots. With his strength fully returned, Link ventured into the Korok Woods and pulled the Master Sword from its pedestal. He then infiltrated the malice-infected Hyrule Castle and battled Calamity Ganon, weakened by the Divine Beasts' combined assault. During one final battle with the Dark Beast Ganon, Zelda bestows Link the Bow of Light and defeats the beast, freeing Zelda from its hold and allowing her to seal him away. Hyrule is saved, and peace is restored.

(A/N: The summary of the upcoming sequel will be added here later! Also, no one is permitted to add to the sequel route until its official release and gameplay.)

The only question is, which of these scenarios will you choose?

Size-Alteration: There are many ways a character from The Legend of Zelda series can shrink and grow. In one scenario, a boy character(s) finds a mushroom spore in a forested area which permanently shrinks anyone who comes into contact with the spore, but can be administered with an antidote. In another scenario, the characters have their sizes altered by Calamity Ganon's malice, from boys within the age limit being shrunken to female characters growing to giant size.

Born Tiny: The young male character(s) are a fraction of the hundreds of children born tiny due to the genetic deficiency of the XY chromosome, so with a world full of predominantly female characters, they are doted on by the giant girls in their lives, or they are seduced by them. In another scenario, a boy is from a race of tiny people based on Breath of the Wild's concept art. The lands of Hyrule and Termina is dangerous enough at normal size, but that danger at their size is multiplied a hundred fold.

Normal-Size Among Giantesses: The male character(s) are born normal-sized, but the female characters in the series are more massive than him. In one scenario, the boy is close friends with/raised by the Great Fairies, who are already gigantic in comparison to a normal-sized Hylian. In another scenario, the women seem to be larger than normal size by an unknown form of magic, with the young girls being the size of the Goron chief, Daruk, and the adults as large as a two-story house in Castle Town Square. In another scenario, the Gerudo become taller than their average normal adult size seen in both games, and they're looking to adopt/play with the male characters who enter their territory.

-or-

Space-Time Rift: The young male character(s) accidentally open or discover a rift in space of time that takes them to anywhere in Hyrule, or into a different era in the past for the future. The only problem is that the rift also has some nasty side-effects which makes the young male character(s) appear as small as a Minish on the other side of the rift and at the mercy of the gigantic female characters!
1531251.title: Great Calamity Timeline

15313.content: Will we be focusing on Original Characters or Actual Canon Characters?
15313.title: Pokémon series

1531311.content:
"This is the life," Jessie sighed as she watched television on her huge flat screen television, reclining in her massive chair while stuffing her face with junk food. Jessie weighed several hundred pounds, each breast bigger than a Minior. She owned a soft doughy belly the size of a large bean bag chair. Jessie's buttocks, hips and thighs were so large that it couldn't fit through most doors, which was why all the doors in her mansion were extra wide.

Now you might be wondering how did Jessie end like this?

Well, after yet another time of being blasted away by Ash and his Pikachu, Jessie ended up being separated from James and Meowth and landing in an abandoned mine. While trying to find a way out, Jessie ended up finding a diamond the size of a Lickilicky. After several hours of struggle and creative thinking, Jessie managed to get it out of mine and sell it for a stupidly high amount of money that would leave Jessie set for the rest of her life. The red head would then go on to abandon Team Rocket to do her own thing and make more money by investing money in the stock market and various businesses. Jessie's days from then on would consist of lazing about eating which over time caused Jessie to bloat up.

Jessie grew lonely having no one to spend her days with. Having know way of finding James and Meowth and knowing the two would never forgive her, Jessie decided to have a child, paying for artificial insemination and eventually having a son named Jeremy, who inherited Jessie's hair and eye color. Unfortunately, there was a side affect with procedure that caused the boy to be born a few inches or so tall, but he loved his massive mother to bits.

How old is Jeremy?
1531311.title: Jumbo Jessie

153132.content:
The boy is a canon character of the Pokémon series.

The Pokémon franchise first started out with Pokémon Red and Green, paired versions Game Boy games released in 1996 that would soon become one of the biggest selling franchises in the world. The franchise would not only include more generational games in the ever-expanding world of Pokémon, but is also adapted into its own manga series based on the storyline of the games, as well as an anime television series featuring Ash Ketchum and Pokémon mascot, Pikachu as they travel around the world catching Pokémon.

In this story route, the routes will be divided into three separate routes. The first route will feature the video game series (i.e. Pokémon Generations I - VIII, spin-off games, etc.); the second route will feature the anime series (i.e. Pokémon - Pokémon Journeys, Pokémon movies, Pokémon Chronicles, etc.); and the third route will feature the manga series (i.e. Pokémon Adventures, spin-offs, etc.)

The only question is, which route will you follow?

Pokémon video games

Pokémon anime series

-or-

Pokémon manga series
153132.title: Canon

1531322.content:
The boy is a canon character from the Pokémon anime series.

In this route, we will be focusing on young male characters exclusive to the anime series.

The only question is, which of these young male characters do we follow?

Ash Ketchum: The young protagonist of the Pokémon anime series who travels the world along with his partner, Pikachu, Ash aspires to become a Pokémon master. Ash is from Pallet Town, where he lived with his mother until he turned ten, the proper age for a Pokémon trainer to start their journey. Along with Pikachu and his other companions, Ash hopes to win every Pokémon League Conference (but only won the one in Alola so far).

Ritchie: A young rival of Ash's in the Indigo Plateau Conference who beat Ash on account that Ash's Charizard refused to fight Ritchie's Pikachu partner, Sparky. Ritchie is a kindhearted boy who, like Ash, travels around the regions to become a Pokémon master and indulges in the occasional Pokémon research and folklore.

Max: The younger brother of May from the Pokémon: Ruby and Sapphire series who journeys with Ash through the Hoenn region and aspires to be a Pokémon trainer. The son of the Petalburg Gym Leader, Max has an encyclopedic knowledge of Pokémon without the use of a Pokédex. Max is currently working and studying to become a Pokémon trainer so he could battle Ash.

Goh: The young duo protagonist from Pokémon Journeys who teams up with Ash to catch every Pokémon in the world. As a younger boy, Goh witnessed the legendary Mew and dreams of becoming a Pokémon trainer so he could catch it. Goh is ambitious, overconfident, calm, and collected in contrast to Ash's ambitious, excitable personality.
1531322.title: Pokémon Anime Series

15314.content:
Super Smash Bros. is the ultimate crossover fighting game series, combining different characters from different Nintendo franchises, as well as third-party universe from non-Nintendo game companies such as Sonic the Hedgehog, Metal Gear Solid, Street Fighter, Bayonetta, and much, much more

The original Super Smash Bros. was released for the Nintendo 64, featuring only eight characters, including the Mario Bros., Donkey Kong, Samus, Pikachu, Jigglypuff Kirby, Fox, Yoshi, Ness, and Captain Falcon. In Super Smash Bros. Melee and since then, the franchise grew to include more characters from their franchises, including antagonistic characters.

Super Smash Bros. Brawl introduced their first third-party universes with the inclusion of Sonic the Hedgehog and Snake from their respective game series, and this was expanded upon in the fourth game and Ultimate with the inclusion of Bandai Namco, CAPCOM, Konami, Microsoft, and other major video game development companies. The rest of the scenarios are reserved for the reader's choice.

In this story route, we'll be exploring the young male characters of the franchise as they're interacting with the gigantic female characters in the franchise, either with the boys being shrunken or the girls being giant-sized. (A/N: You can also include previous designs of characters from the previous Super Smash Bros. games as separate characters, such as Zelda. Female Assist Trophies such as Lyn and Barbara the Bat are also included in this route.) The rest of the scenarios are reserved for the reader's choice.

The only question is, which route will you choose?

Shrunken: In this route's scenarios, the young male characters in the franchise have been shrunken after obtaining the Poison Mushroom OR the female characters have found a way to bypass the Custom Smash rules so they would remain normal-sized while the boys remain tiny-sized and are easily subjected to their whims.

Giant: In this route's scenarios, the female characters appearing in the franchise have grown into giant size after obtaining the Super Mushroom OR they have found a way to bypass the Custom Smash rules so they would remain giant-sized while the boys are still normal-sized but are easily subjected to their whims.
15314.title: Super Smash Bros. series

153141.content:
The young male characters from Super Smash Bros. Ultimate have been shrunken down!

The only question is, who is shrunken down, and how?

Ness/Lucas: While practicing a new form of PK power for their upcoming matches, Ness (and possibly including Lucas) is shrunken down to an inch tall! What's worse is that the young EarthBound representative(s) is being pulled into a match featuring their female friends from the other franchises! When the girls find out, they take in the young boy(s) and keep them safe.

Young Link: During a match, Young Link accidentally touches a Poison Mushroom, and he is shrunken down as the match ends! If that isn't bad enough, he is then trapped in the girls' dormitory of the expansive Smash Mansion with no sign of him growing back! Young Link must grab the giant girls' attention, or he will be shrunken forever!

Male Pokémon Trainer: The male Pokémon Trainer is feeling proud and flaunts that he is able to switch his Pokémon midbattle and avoid certain damage to his beloved comrades. His female counterpart decides that he should be knocked down a peg, so she slips a Poison Mushroom in his drink and shrinks him down, where she and the female fighters and Assist Trophies should teach him alesson in humility.

-or-

Pit/Dark Pit: Bayonetta, in an effort to appease Madama Butterfly, her Infernal Demon contractor, alters the Custom Smash rules so that the young angel(s) would be shrunken and not her. The flamboyant Umbra Witch takes the angel(s) hostage and uses them for her pleasure, sharing that pleasure with her lover, the Goddess Palutena.
153141.title: Shrunken

choices: Bowser Jr. and the Male Koopalings
1531415.content:
The young male characters from Super Smash Bros. Ultimate have been shrunken down!

The only question is, who is shrunken down, and how?

Bowser Jr. and the Male Koopalings: In a sneak attempt to capture the princess from the various Nintendo franchises, Bowser Jr. and the male Koopalings use a Lightning to shrink them down. Unfortunately, things go wrong when Iggy Koopa accidentally uses the Lightning, and shrinks them down! They're either shrunken around Wendy or the princesses; either way, they're turtle toys for the titanesses!

Villager: Despite the monotonus faces and their status in the Animal Crossing series, the Villager is still a young male character. In this route, a Villager is sick and takes one of Dr. Mario's Megavitamins when his illness got worse. The next morning, the Villager is shrunken down as a side effect, and even worse is when the lovable Shih Tzu Isabelle is infatuated by how adorable he was.

Male Inklings: The male Inklings, in their Squid form, squeezes too much ink, and they've been shrunken down to a tiny size. Even though they can still use their Squid forms to climb up inked surfaces, the male Inklings are in danger from the looming threat of the giantesses of Smash mansion... and some of them would prefer to have squid for lunch.
1531415.title: Continuing with Shrunken

153142.content:
The female characters from Super Smash Bros. Ultimate have grown into giant size!

The only question is, who grows into a giant, and how?

The Nintendo Princesses: The princess from their respective Nintendo franchises have accidentally ingested the Super Mushroom, so they grow into giant princesses! With their size, they try to flaunt their new size to the boys, but the only ones in the Smash Mansion are the ones younger than the male Pokémon trainer.

Zero Suit Samus: During a match, Zero Suit Samus grabs a Lightning, which makes turns her into a giant just as the match ends! If that isn't bad enough, Zero Suit Samus doesn't show any sign of shrinking back down to normal size! What's worse is that a few of the young male characters she views as surrogate sons are in constant danger while she's gigantic!

Palutena: While attempting to cook her own dish, Palutena is distracted while she adds in a growth serum to enhance the flavor, only to add in too much. When she consumes the dish, her body grows bigger than Smash Mansion, like the goddess she is. Pit openly shows his devotion by worshipping her, and the Goddess encourages his young friends to do the same.

-or-

Bayonetta: Bayonetta, in an effort to appease Madama Butterfly, her Infernal Demon contractor, alters the Custom Smash rules so that she would be the size of the Infernal Demon herself. The flamboyant Umbra Witch takes the young male competitors hostage and uses them for her pleasure, eventually allowing other female fighters to join.
153142.title: Giant

1531425.content:
The female characters from Super Smash Bros. Ultimate have grown into giant size!

The only question is, who grows into a giant, and how?

Wendy O. Koopa and Isabelle: Sick of being little in comparison to everyone else, the short-fused diva Wendy O. Koopa and the lovable Shih Tzu Isabelle each receives Super Mushroom in their room, along with a customization that keeps them giant-sized forever. Now the two anthropomorphic girls battle it out in a classic good vs. evil kaiju battle with the Smash mansion and the fighters on the line!

Female Wii Fit Trainer: The monotonous Wii Fit Trainer is known for keeping her friends in shape and fit, but she starts to feel frustrated since the younger male contestants have skipped her exercise and yoga sessions. To rectify this, she alters the Custom Smash rules so she is gigantic and forces the boys into an exercise regimen that they'll never forget.

Female Inkling: The female Inklings absorb too much ink, causing them to grow to gigantic sizes. Using their size, the female Inklings then play with the other younger male characters, including the male Inklings, so they're subjected to their own whims.

-or-

Min Min: The Chinese ARMS fighter Min Min eats a bowl of ramen, but she unknowingly slips a few sliced Super Mushrooms in her noodles, so each Super Mushroom she consumes, she grows bigger, as does her belly. She becomes a rampaging, hungry giantess who will devour anything and anyone deemed edible, including her young male friends.
1531425.title: Continuing with Giant

15315.content:
The boy is a character from a video game created and produced by Nintendo.

In this route, we will be focusing on certain boy characters appearing in Nintendo's other well-known franchises, such as Pit (from the Kid Icarus series), or an original character of your choice within the canon or non-canon Nintendo game universes. They will be shrunken, born tiny, or every woman in the Nintendo games is bigger than the normal-sized character.

The only question is, which of these other Nintendo franchises will you choose?

F-Zero series

Splatoon series

Kid Icarus series

-or-

Fire Emblem series
15315.title: Continuing with Nintendo I

1535.content:
The boy is a character from a video game or video game franchise.

In this route, we will be focusing on certain boy characters appearing in other well-known video game franchises, such as Frisk (from Undertale), or an original character of your choice within the canon or non-canon video game universes. They will be shrunken, born tiny, or every woman in the video games is bigger than the normal-sized character.

The only question is, which of these games will you choose?

Bayonetta series

Undertale series

Street Fighter series

-or-

Mortal Kombat series
1535.title: Continuing with Video Games I

15352.content:
The boy is a character from the Undertale series.

The Undertale series is known for its divided routes, its iconic characters, and for borrowing the Earthbound-style RPG battle system.

In Undertale, a child explores the cave of Mt. Ebott where he falls down a hole and into an underground society of monsters where he encounters the evil flower, Flowey. Depending on the actions the player takes, the child either makes it through the entire monster world peacefully while complimenting the monsters and dodging their attacks, or the child goes on a genocide spree and kills every monster in sight.

In Deltarune, a child named Kris lives in a world where monsters and humans live harmoniously in society. Kris and female monster delinquent, Susie, are sent to a Dark World where they ally themselves with the young prince, Ralsei to defeat the King who wants to spread darkness outside of the Dark World using the Dark Fountain.

Both of these games are very much similar with each other in terms of characters and RPG battle systems.

The only question is, which of these games will you choose to follow?
15352.title: Undertale series

153521.content:
The boy is a character from Undertale.

Many years ago, monsters and humans, once equal with each other, were banished into the Underground. The barrier to the Underground was sealed by a magic barrier, with the only point of entrance being the cave within Mt. Ebott.

In Undertale, a human child named Frisk wanders into the cave in Mt. Ebott and falls into the Underground where Frisk encounters the evil sentient flower, Flowey, who teaches him about the game's mechanics and encourages him to gain "LV", or "LOVE", and gain "EXP" by defeating monsters. It's at this point in the beginning of the story where the child's route will take them.

In the Genocide route, Frisk gains "LOVE" (an acronym for Level of Violence) and "EXP" (an acronym for Execution Points) by killing the monsters. Under the influence of Chara, the spirit of a the first fallen human child, Frisk kills all of the monsters and goes on to destroy the universe.

In the Pacifist route, Frisk defies Flowey by not killing the monsters he encounters, but is forced to fight him when he captured the SOULs of the humans and monsters, and emerges triumphant. Flowey is revealed to be Asirel, the reincarnated son of King Asgore and Queen Toriel, and expressed his remorse for his actions by destroying the barrier separating the monsters and the humans. Frisk is then given the option to be Toriel's adopted son.

In this route, we will be focusing on certain boy characters appearing in Undertale, such as Frisk, Chara, Asriel Dreemurr, or the Monster Kid. They will be shrunken, born tiny, or every female character in the game is bigger than the normal-sized character.

The only question is, which of these characters will you choose to follow?

Frisk

Chara

Asriel Dreemurr

-or-

Monster Kid
153521.title: Undertale

1535215.content: Or YOU fall into Underground.
1535215.title: Other

15355.content:
The boy is a character from a video game, or video game franchise.

In this route, we will be focusing on certain boy characters appearing in other well-known video game franchises, such as Timmy (from the Overwatch cinematic), or an original character of your choice within the canon or non-canon video game universes. They will be shrunken, born tiny, or every woman in the video games is bigger than the normal-sized character.

The only question is, which of these games will you choose?

Overwatch

Persona series

God of War series

-or-

Mass Effect series
15355.title: Continuing with Video Games II

choices: The Bite-Sized Brothers of Overwatch’s Mascot
The Mighty Minuscule Recruit of Overwatch
153551.content:
Overwatch is a popular online multiplayer video game, featuring a roster of playable heroes and villains with a variety of abilities.

Overwatch takes place in a cybernetic future where the world was threatened by the terrorist organization, Talon, and the growing tensions between the humans and the robotic Omnics. To combat these threats, Overwatch was established with the intention of giving service to the civilians, ending any villainy across the world, and becoming heroes. Unfortunately, all that came to an end when the world's governments disbanded Overwatch, threatening to indict any heroes who step into the light.

In spite of all this, some of the former members of Overwatch resumed their heroics, secretly in uniform, or publicly as a do-gooder citizen. When the world saw itself immersed in constant combined threats of Talon and the rogue Omnics, the members of Overwatch were called back into action, inspiring new heroes to come out of the shadows and join the fight.

In this story route, we will be focusing on two certain boys that first appeared in the Overwatch cinematic trailer: Brian, and his younger brother, Timmy. In the cinematic trailer, the two brothers were visiting the Overwatch museum when they became witnesses to a fight between two of Overwatch's former members, Tracer and Winston, and two of Talon's heavy-hitting mercenaries, Reaper and Widowmaker over the claim of Doomfist's powerful gauntlet.

In the climax of this battle, Brian aided Overwatch's victory by knocking out Widowmaker with Doomfist's gauntlet, thus ensuring Talon's defeat as the final use of the gauntlet ultimately damaged and depowered it. While the Talon agents retreated and Winston pursued the villains, Tracer told the boys, "the world could always use more heroes" and returned the gauntlet back to its exhibit display. That speech and her salute inspired Brian that day, and an excited Timmy wouldn't stop talking about it for a few weeks.

Six months later, Brian and Timmy were out running errands at the shopping mall. Unfortunately, that was the day Talon decided to display their terror by striking the mall, using Omnics controlled by the hacker, Sombra and the loyal Talon soldiers to massacre the poor civilians caught in the line of fire, including Brian and Timmy's parents. At the end of that horrible bloodbath, the Talon agents were ordered by Talon's chief mad scientist, Moira to take any survivors they could and bring them so she would perform her experiments on them.

Brian and Timmy were caught trying to slip away from the mall, being the youngest survivors to be captured among a throng of other traumatized victims forced into aerial carrier at gunpoint. Hours later, the Talon air carrier arrived at their base of operations, located in a blizzardy mountain range. The Talon agents forced the boys to sit in a cramped cell while Moira observed her victims, accompanied by Widowmaker and Sombra. Almost immediately, Brian was recognized by Widowmaker and became the target of her wrath for denying her victory at the museum, but Moira stopped her and closely observed the boys' vitals. The brothers earned Moira's attention and intrigue.

Moira decided the boys would be perfect for her latest experiment. She stuck the boys into a large chamber where a radioactive veil passed through them, shrinking them down to tiny size, the size of a child's thumb. While Moira subjected the two young shrinkees to her experiments, Widowmaker subjected Brian to spiteful torture, and Sombra decided to use Timmy ar her own personal plaything, seeing how she was attracted to his cuteness and innocence. It was there that they discovered that the chamber's shrinking radiation granted the boys high physical durability and immunity to stomach acids. Upon this discovery, the brothers were subjected to even more torture and playing for two straight months.

The two months passed too slowly for the boys as they were at the mercy of the giant villainesses' whims. After the second month passed, salvation finally came for the traumatized shrunken boys when the female members of Overwatch took the Talon base by surprise, resulting in the rescue of three-quarters of the prisoners still alive. Tracer, who led the charge, found the tiny boys locked away in Moira’s office, caged in a solid energy cube. Tracer and her comrades freed them and she and the girls spent their aftermath comforting the grieving boys.

Not much later, it was decided by Overwatch that Tracer would adopt Brian and Timmy as her younger brothers and allowed them to stay with her and her partner at their apartment. In addition, the boys had to attend weekly psychiatric sessions with Mercy to help them with their psychological trauma from witnessing the massacre of their family and being imprisoned and tortured by Talon. Meanwhile, the oys would be educated by the brilliant minds of Mei, D.Va, and Efi Oladele (with whom Timmy falls in love with) to make up for the months of formal education lost in captivity.

A few months passed as Brian and Timmy became a part of Tracer's family and regularly attended Mercy's psychiatric sessions. Brian decided the only way for him to overcome his fear is to become brave and train to become a member of Overwatch. Through training from various female members, and with the help of Hammond the mechanical genius hamster, Brian is recruited into Overwatch as its youngest member to join. With the help of his new family and Timmy's undying support, Brian is determined to avenge his fallen parents and defeat the organization responsible.

There are three parts of the boys' lives that are being focused on: Their captivity in the Talon base, their lives living with Tracer and her partner, and Brian's training into Overwatch. The only question is, which of these three parts will you choose?

1.) The boys’ time as Talon’s youngest prisoners; their shrinkage and torture.

2.) The boys living with Tracer and her partner as their adopted little brothers.

3.) Brian training with the female Overwatch members to become their youngest and smallest recruit.
153551.title: Overwatch

choices: Two Months Later, Overwatch Rescues the Boys
1535511.content:
It was supposed to be a good day for the young brothers, Brian and Timmy while they and their family as they were all shopping at the shopping mall. But things changed when the terrorist organization, Talon attacked.

Timmy and Brian remembered that moment when the Omnics, war machines hacked by Talon, caught their parents in the line of fire. They were among two of the two hundred expendable victims rained down by bullets from Talon’s men and machines. Timmy and Brian took cover in the shadows cast by the pillars, the benches, and the potted plants, helplessly watching as the civilians were mercilessly slaughtered by Talon, hearing their victims scream in terror and falling silently to the overwhelming gunfire.

Brian took a peek and spotted a phone on the other side of his hiding area. Knowing what had to be done, he firmly instructed Timmy to remain in his hiding area as he went for the phones. Stealthy as Brian was, when he tried contacting the police for help, his whispers attracted the attention of two Talon soldiers aiming their weapons at him. Timmy gasped too loudly, and by the time he covered his mouth, the Talon soldiers moved in on him, their weapons raised at him.

Just before someone pulled the trigger, one of the Talon soldiers stopped the other from shooting the boy and received an order to take any survivor back to their base. The Talon soldiers then grabbed the brothers by the arms and dragged them away kicking and screaming from the scene of their family's slaughter. They, among dozens of other crying survivors, were forced outside by gunpoint and taken to an aerial mobile carrier in the parking lot. Out of the victims who were taken, Timmy and Brian were the youngest.

The aerial carrier carried the kidnapped victims to Talon’s secret base somewhere in a snowy mountain range, where exactly, no one could tell. When the ship arrived at the base, the victims were forcefully disembarked and led into a dark corridor, thrown into energy cells designed to prevent them from escaping. The boys were surrounded by dark armored assailants with weapons and the dozens of people crying for the trauma of the wanton carnage, the deaths of their friends and families, or the fear for their lives. Timmy cried for all of the above while Brian could only hold him close to him for comfort.

Not long after they were locked, the doors opened, and through that door came three of the dangerous women in Talon: Moira, the Irish mad scientist; Sombra, the Spanish hacker and chameleon; and Widowmaker, the French femme fatale sniper. Timmy gasped and curled up in a ball, and Brian pulled his hood over his head hoping that Widowmaker wouldn’t notice him because he was there when Brian slugged her with Doomfist’s gauntlet, the very weapon which she sought to ascertain and failed to do so.

“This is a lovely batch of test subjects our troops have collected,” Moira observed, admiring the variety of poor victims in her own sadistic glee. “Each of these subjects has the potential to become a part of something grand, something that will not only defeat Overwatch once and for all, but expand the science of borders to new horizons. For this, we need only choose one for our molecular compression veil. Now, who shall it be?”

As the three villainesses passed their cell, Brian lifted his head only an inch to partially show his face, his eyes briefly meeting with Widowmaker's. He quickly averted his gaze, but the sound of high heels striding towards his cell confirmed his fear. “You zhere, boy!” Widowmaker called, banging on the cell’s energy field. “Yes, you, I seem to recall zhat face! Show eet to me now!”

With one giant bang against the field, Timmy hugged against his brother tighter. Brian complied and showed the French assassin his face, and suddenly, a heated Widowmaker unsheathed her signature rifle, and aimed it at the boys. “Zhat face! I know who you are, boy! Zhen zhe other one is...!"

Brian lifted his head only an inch to partially show his face, and Widowmaker's fury increased. "I knew it! You are zhe boy who humiliated me in zhe museum! You ruined our operation to retrieve Doomfist’s gauntlet! Now you must pay!”

“Hold on, so that chico’s the boy who slugged you with Doomfist’s gauntlet?” Sombra asked, suddenly bursting out with laughter. “Oh, dama araña, that must have been embarrassing for you!”

“Mord ta langue, Sombra!” Widowmaker barked at the Spanish hacker. “He humiliated me, and now he and his brother will pay for their crimes dearly!"

Being someone with no remorse, Widowmaker had no qualms with killing kids, especially one who was involved with her failure for her mission to retrieve Doomfist’s gauntlet. Just as she was about to squeeze the trigger, a clawed hand belonging to the skinny redheaded belonging to Moira gently shoved the gun away. “As much as I enjoy a revenge killing from time to time, Widowmaker, I would advise restraining your impulses for the time being,” Moira said, approaching the boy’s cell. “Right now, I would personally like to inspect the boy.”

Moira nodded her head towards one of the Talon soldiers, and the soldier disabled the energy field. The soldiers restrained the boys, and Moira grabbed each of them by the arm. “Ow! Owww! Please, let me go! You’re hurting me!” Timmy begged, thrashing in the soldier's grip to break free.

“Quit struggling, boy. I am only going to inspect your vitals,” Moira said as she pulled up a holovid medical file on Timmy.

Timmy sobbed and cried while Brian remained defiantly silent. His narrowed eyes met with Widowmaker's, and both of them exchanged the same animosity towards each other for a different reason. “Hmmm… oh… I see…” Moira swiped the holovid away and closely inspected the boy’s sobbing face, holding his cheeks with one hand underneath his chin. “Ahhh… intriguing. Very intriguing. And now you, young one."

Moira pulled up another holovid medical, this time on Brian. She hummed curiously, then contently, a satisfied smirk spread across her facial features. "Yes... perfect.
